title: heracleum sphondylium subsp. verticillatum
intro: Heracleum sphondylium subsp. verticillatum, also known as wild celery, is a subspecies of wild parsnip that belongs to the carrot family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, thanks to its high content of essential oils and bioactive compounds. Wild celery can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, making it a versatile remedy in traditional medicine.
